title: leucanthemum pyrenaicum
intro: Leucanthemum pyrenaicum, also known as Pyrenean oxeye daisy, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to the Pyrenees and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. Leucanthemum pyrenaicum is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
